{"credits": [{"type": "reporter", "user": {"avatar_url": "https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/33688944?v=4", "events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/events{/privacy}", "followers_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/followers", "following_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/following{/other_user}", "gists_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/gists{/gist_id}", "gravatar_id": "", "html_url": "https://github.com/ik0z", "id": 33688944, "login": "ik0z", "node_id": "MDQ6VXNlcjMzNjg4OTQ0", "organizations_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/orgs", "received_events_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/received_events", "repos_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/repos", "site_admin": false, "starred_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/starred{/owner}{/repo}", "subscriptions_url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z/subscriptions", "type": "User", "url": "https://api.github.com/users/ik0z", "user_view_type": "public"}}], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-46551", "cvss": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 6.5, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:N/I:N/A:H"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-770", "name": "Allocation of Resources Without Limits or Throttling"}], "description": "### Summary

The `uploadViaURL` path in the v1/v2 attachment API did not enforce `NC_ATTACHMENT_FIELD_SIZE` against the remote `content-length` or against the response stream. An authenticated user (Editor+) could direct the server to download arbitrarily large files, exhausting disk space and causing denial of service.

### Details

In `packages/nocodb/src/services/attachments.service.ts`, the HEAD probe read `content-length` but never compared it to `NC_ATTACHMENT_FIELD_SIZE`; the subsequent `storageAdapter.fileCreateByUrl()` performed the download without `maxContentLength`. The v3 service (`v3/data-attachment-v3.service.ts`) already enforced the limit, but the v1/v2 endpoints (`POST /api/v1/db/storage/upload-by-url`, `POST /api/v2/storage/upload-by-url`) did not.

This is distinct from GHSA-xr7v-j379-34v9 (blind SSRF via HEAD) — same code area, different class.

### Impact

- Authenticated DoS via disk exhaustion. Editor role suffices.
- Cascading failures once disk fills: blocked DB writes, log rotation, application crash.

### Credit

This issue was reported by [@ik0z](https://github.com/ik0z).", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00235, "percentile": 0.1413}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-21T20:35:41Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-46551"}], "nvd_published_at": null, "published_at": "2026-05-21T20:35:41Z", "references": ["https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb/security/advisories/GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/nocodb/nocodb/security-advisories/GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p", "severity": "medium", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/nocodb/nocodb", "summary": "NocoDB: Missing File Size Enforcement in Upload-by-URL Allows Denial of Service via Disk Exhaustion", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-21T20:35:42Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-99vc-2jx2-688p", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": null, "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "nocodb"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "<= 0.301.3"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

title: NocoDB: Missing File Size Enforcement in Upload-by-URL Allows Denial of Service via Disk Exhaustion
